constructor.name: CustomOption
constructor.comment: Creates an Option with a given specification and a default arguments processor.

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param argName the name of the single argument to be passed to the option, used in the help message

constructor.name: CustomOption
constructor.comment: Creates an Option with a given specification.

method.name: newOneArg
method.comment: Creates an Option with a single argument value and a given specification.

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param argName the name of the single argument to be passed to the option, used in the help message

method.name: newNoArgs
method.comment: Creates an Option with no arguments and a given specification

method.name: getNames
method.comment: {@inheritDoc}, In the case of this class, the list has only one element, the single option name.

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return a list with a single element containing the name of the option

method.name: getName
method.comment: This class provides a single name for the option. Therefore, no alternative names are supported.

tag.name: return

method.name: process
method.comment: {@inheritDoc}, It must check if the given option arguments are valid.

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param arguments {@inheritDoc} received during the doclet execution

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return if the option has the expected number of arguments and they are valid

field.name: name
field.comment: The name of the option, such as ,{@code d}, or ,{@code debug},, without a preceding hyphen (included automatically).

field.name: parameters
field.comment:
A user-friendly string description of the option's parameters (arguments), or the empty string if this option has no parameters. This is used to generate the help message for the option. If the option expectes a file name as parameter, the description may include the word "file" to indicate that.

If the option has multiple parameters, this attribute should provide a string representation of all of them, such as: file1 file2

field.name: argumentsProcessor
field.comment: A function (,{@link java.util.function.BiPredicate},) to validate the option arguments given in the command line. The function can check the number of required arguments, try to convert them to the expected type and perform any validation required. If the arguments are valid, the Predicate must return true. The first parameter of the Predicate is the option name and the second one is the list of arguments.

field.name: argumentCount
field.comment: The number of arguments this option will consume, which is the number of expected ,{@link #parameters},.

class.name: Parser
class.comment: The main parser class. It scans the given Doclet document root and creates the XML tree.

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param env the operating environment of a single invocation of the doclet

method.name: getJavaDoc
method.comment:
Gets the JavaDoc comment for an element.
This method retrieves the DocCommentTree for an element and returns its full body as a string. It also post-processes the comment to fix an issue where HTML entities and tags get commas added around them. The regex replacements remove these unwanted commas to ensure the HTML content is properly preserved in the generated XML.

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param element the element to get its JavaDoc

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return the element's JavaDoc comment or empty string if none exists

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return the tags inside a JavaDoc comment

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param element the Java element to get its JavaDoc tags

method.name: parseRootDoc
method.comment: The entry point into parsing the javadoc.

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The root node, containing everything parsed from javadoc doclet

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return the package node for the given class element

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param classElement class to get its package

method.name: getTopLevelClass
method.comment: {@return the top-level class of a given inner class, or the class itself if it's not an inner class}

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param classElement a class or inner class

method.name: parseAnnotationTypeDoc
method.comment: Parse the JavaDoc of an annotation type.

method.name: parseAnnotationTypeElementDoc
method.comment: Parse the elements of an annotation

method.name: parseEnumConstant
method.comment: Parses an enum type definition

method.name: parseTypeParameter
method.comment: Parse type variables for generics

method.name: parseTypeParameterBound
method.comment: Gets a type parameter bound for a generic type (such as <T extends Number> or <T extends Comparable<E> & Serializable>) and splits the name of each type into a list of strings

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return a list of strings representing each type parameter bound

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return string representation of the element scope

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param doc the element to get its scope

method.name: parseTypeInfo
method.comment: Parses a ,{@link TypeMirror}, into a ,{@link TypeInfo}, object used by the XmlDoclet.

field.name: packages
field.comment: A map where each key is a package name and each value is an object containing a package's JavaDoc.

field.name: env
field.comment: The operating environment of a single invocation of the doclet

method.name: hasModifier
method.comment: Checks if an element has a given modifier

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param modifier the modifier we are looking for in the element

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return true if the modifier is present in the element declaration, false otherwise

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return the list of fields from a given class element

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param classElement the class to get its fields

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return the list of constructors from a given class element

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param classElement the class to get its constructors

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return the list of methods from a given class element

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param classElement the class to get its methods

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return a type as WildcardType if it is such a type, or null otherwise

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param typeMirror the type to get it as a wildcard type

method.name: getParameterizedType
method.comment: Gets a type as DeclaredType if the typeMirror has type arguments (such a ,{@code List<String>},).

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param typeMirror the type to get it as a wildcard type

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return the type as DeclaredType if it has type arguments, or null otherwise

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return the dimension of type that represents an array, or an empty string if the type is not an array

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param typeMirror the array type to get its dimension

method.name: getEnumConstants
method.comment: Gets the enum constants from a TypeElement that represents an enum type.

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param enumTypeElement the TypeElement representing the enum type

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return a list of VariableElement representing the enum constants

method.name: run
method.comment: Processes the JavaDoc documentation. This method is required for all doclets.

tag.name: param
tag.text: @param env the operating environment of a single invocation of the doclet

method.name: save
method.comment: Save XML object model to a file via JAXB.

field.name: root
field.comment: The parsed object model. Used in unit tests.

class.name: Annotation
class.comment:
Java class for annotation complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.

{@code
 
   
     
       
         
         
         
         
       
       
       
       
       
     
   
 
 }

method.name: getComment
method.comment: Gets the value of the comment property.

method.name: setComment
method.comment: Sets the value of the comment property.

method.name: getTag
method.comment:
Gets the value of the tag property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the tag property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getTag().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link TagInfo},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the tag property.

method.name: getElement
method.comment:
Gets the value of the element property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the element property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getElement().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link AnnotationElement},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the element property.

method.name: getAnnotation
method.comment:
Gets the value of the annotation property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the annotation property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getAnnotation().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link AnnotationInstance},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the annotation property.

method.name: getName
method.comment: Gets the value of the name property.

method.name: setName
method.comment: Sets the value of the name property.

method.name: getQualified
method.comment: Gets the value of the qualified property.

method.name: setQualified
method.comment: Sets the value of the qualified property.

method.name: getScope
method.comment: Gets the value of the scope property.

method.name: setScope
method.comment: Sets the value of the scope property.

method.name: isIncluded
method.comment: Gets the value of the included property.

method.name: setIncluded
method.comment: Sets the value of the included property.

class.name: AnnotationArgument
class.comment:
Java class for annotationArgument complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.

{@code
 
   
     
       
         
         
           
           
         
       
       
       
       
     
   
 
 }

method.name: getType
method.comment: Gets the value of the type property.

method.name: setType
method.comment: Sets the value of the type property.

method.name: getValue
method.comment:
Gets the value of the value property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the value property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getValue().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link String},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the value property.

method.name: getAnnotation
method.comment:
Gets the value of the annotation property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the annotation property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getAnnotation().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link AnnotationInstance},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the annotation property.

method.name: getName
method.comment: Gets the value of the name property.

method.name: setName
method.comment: Sets the value of the name property.

method.name: isPrimitive
method.comment: Gets the value of the primitive property.

method.name: setPrimitive
method.comment: Sets the value of the primitive property.

method.name: isArray
method.comment: Gets the value of the array property.

method.name: setArray
method.comment: Sets the value of the array property.

method.name: getArgument
method.comment:
Gets the value of the argument property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the argument property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getArgument().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link AnnotationArgument},

tag.text: @return The value of the argument property.

method.name: getComment
method.comment: Gets the value of the comment property.

method.name: setComment
method.comment: Sets the value of the comment property.

method.name: getTag
method.comment:
Gets the value of the tag property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the tag property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getTag().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link TagInfo},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the tag property.

method.name: getGeneric
method.comment:
Gets the value of the generic property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the generic property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getGeneric().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link TypeParameter},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the generic property.

method.name: getClazz
method.comment: Gets the value of the clazz property.

method.name: setClazz
method.comment: Sets the value of the clazz property.

method.name: getInterface
method.comment:
Gets the value of the interface property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the interface property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getInterface().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link TypeInfo},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the interface property.

method.name: getConstructor
method.comment:
Gets the value of the constructor property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the constructor property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getConstructor().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link Constructor},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the constructor property.

method.name: getMethod
method.comment:
Gets the value of the method property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the method property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getMethod().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link Method},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the method property.

method.name: getAnnotation
method.comment:
Gets the value of the annotation property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the annotation property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getAnnotation().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link AnnotationInstance},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the annotation property.

method.name: getField
method.comment:
Gets the value of the field property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the field property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getField().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link Field},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the field property.

method.name: getName
method.comment: Gets the value of the name property.

method.name: setName
method.comment: Sets the value of the name property.

method.comment: Gets the value of the qualified property.

method.comment: Gets the value of the abstract property.

class.name: Constructor
class.comment:
Java class for constructor complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.

{@code
 
   
     
       
         
         
         
         
         
       
       
       
       
       
       
       
       
       
       
       
     
   
 
 }

method.name: getComment
method.comment: Gets the value of the comment property.

method.name: setComment
method.comment: Sets the value of the comment property.

method.name: getTag
method.comment:
Gets the value of the tag property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the tag property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getTag().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link TagInfo},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the tag property.

method.name: getParameter
method.comment:
Gets the value of the parameter property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the parameter property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getParameter().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link MethodParameter},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the parameter property.

method.name: getException
method.comment:
Gets the value of the exception property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the exception property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getException().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link TypeInfo},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the exception property.

method.name: getAnnotation
method.comment:
Gets the value of the annotation property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the annotation property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getAnnotation().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link AnnotationInstance},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the annotation property.

method.name: getComment
method.comment: Gets the value of the comment property.

method.name: setComment
method.comment: Sets the value of the comment property.

method.name: getTag
method.comment:
Gets the value of the tag property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the tag property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getTag().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link TagInfo},

method.name: getTag
method.comment:
Gets the value of the tag property.

This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the tag property.

For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

 getTag().add(newItem);

Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ,{@link TagInfo},

tag.name: return
tag.text: @return The value of the tag property.

class.name: ObjectFactory
class.comment:
This object contains factory methods for each Java content interface and Java element interface generated in the com.manticore.tools.xmldoclet.xjc package.

An ObjectFactory allows you to programmatically construct new instances of the Java representation for XML content. The Java representation of XML content can consist of schema derived interfaces and classes representing the binding of schema type definitions, element declarations and model groups. Factory methods for each of these are provided in this class.

constructor.name: ObjectFactory
constructor.comment: Create a new ObjectFactory that can be used to create new instances of schema derived classes for package: com.manticore.tools.xmldoclet.xjc

method.name: createRoot
method.comment: Create an instance of ,{@link Root}
